region_of_origin: India / Lebanon (fusion platter served in Singapore)
food_subgroup: Paneer-based curry with legume dip and flatbread

ontology.foodon_gap_note: No exact FoodOn entry for Mutter Paneer with Hummus and Lebanese Bread as a combined platter. FOODON_03302050 (curry dish) used as closest parent for the paneer component. Hummus and Lebanese bread components lack dedicated FoodOn entries.
ontology.foodex2_description: Dishes based on legumes and vegetables (closest parent for combined Indian-Middle Eastern platter)
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A0CKP (dishes based on legumes and vegetables) used as closest defensible parent. No exact FoodEx2 code exists for this specific fusion platter combining mutter paneer, hummus, and Lebanese bread.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No suitable SNOMED CT food concept found for this specific fusion platter combination.

serving.description: 1 combo platter (1 portion mutter paneer + 1 portion hummus + 2 pieces Lebanese bread)
serving.natural_language_portion: One serving consists of approximately 200 g of mutter paneer curry, 100 g of hummus, and 250 g (2 pieces) of Lebanese flatbread, totalling approximately 550 g.

nutrient_source.title: Composite estimate: Mutter Paneer (ICMR-NIN Indian Food Composition Tables 2017, item 'Peas and Paneer Curry'), Hummus (USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy #16167 'Hummus, commercial'), Lebanese Bread (USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy #18033 'Bread, pita, white, enriched')
nutrient_source.organization: ICMR-NIN (Indian Council of Medical Research – National Institute of Nutrition); USDA Agricultural Research Service
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No single approved source covers this exact three-component platter. Nutrient values are weighted composites: mutter paneer ~36% by weight (200 g of 550 g total) from ICMR-NIN IFCT 2017; hummus ~18% by weight (100 g) from USDA SR Legacy #16167; Lebanese bread ~46% by weight (250 g) from USDA SR Legacy #18033. Per-100g values derived from weighted average of the three components at their respective proportions.

gi_source.title: Composite GI estimate: Mutter Paneer analogous to 'Curry, vegetable, with legumes' (GI ~41, Atkinson et al. 2008); Hummus analogous to 'Hummus' (GI ~6, University of Sydney GI Database); Lebanese Bread analogous to 'Pita bread, white' (GI ~57, University of Sydney GI Database)
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database; Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC. International Tables of Glycemic Index and Glycemic Load Values: 2008. Diabetes Care.
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for this combined platter. Weighted composite GI calculated from three components: mutter paneer (200 g, GI ~41, carb ~8 g per 100 g → GL contribution), hummus (100 g, GI ~6, carb ~14 g per 100 g), Lebanese/pita bread (250 g, GI ~57, carb ~55 g per 100 g). Weighted GI = sum(GI_i × carb_i × weight_i) / sum(carb_i × weight_i) ≈ 52. GL per serving = (GI × total available carbohydrate in serving) / 100 = (52 × 69.7 g) / 100 ≈ 36. All component GI values sourced from University of Sydney GI Database and Atkinson et al. 2008 (Diabetes Care).

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium content (~310 mg per 100 g; ~1705 mg per serving)
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should be cautious with this platter as a single serving provides approximately 1705 mg sodium (~74% of the 2300 mg daily limit), primarily from the hummus and spiced paneer curry; portion reduction or requesting low-sodium preparation is advisable.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Moderate glycemic load (~36 per serving) driven by Lebanese bread
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes should note that the Lebanese bread component contributes the majority of the glycemic load (GL ~36 per full serving); reducing bread to one piece and pairing with the high-fibre hummus and paneer curry can help moderate postprandial glucose response.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat from paneer (~2.9 g per 100 g) and moderate sodium load
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: For individuals with cardiovascular disease, this platter provides beneficial unsaturated fats from olive oil in hummus and dietary fibre from chickpeas and peas, but the saturated fat from paneer and the high sodium content warrant portion control and preference for smaller paneer servings.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Mutter Paneer with Hummus and Lebanese Bread is a fusion platter combining two distinct culinary traditions: North Indian vegetarian cuisine and Levantine mezze. Mutter paneer is a classic North Indian curry made by simmering green peas (mutter) and cubes of fresh Indian cottage cheese (paneer) in a spiced tomato-onion gravy seasoned with cumin, coriander, garam masala, ginger, and garlic. The dish is rich, mildly spiced, and creamy due to the paneer. Hummus is a smooth, protein-rich dip made from blended cooked chickpeas, tahini (sesame paste), lemon juice, garlic, and olive oil, originating from the Levant region. Lebanese bread (also called Lebanese flatbread or pita-style bread) is a soft, leavened wheat flatbread baked in a hot oven, used as a vehicle for scooping the hummus and curry. Together, the platter provides a nutritionally complementary meal: the paneer and chickpeas supply complete and plant-based protein respectively, the peas and chickpeas contribute dietary fibre and folate, and the olive oil in hummus provides heart-healthy monounsaturated fats. The combined serving (~550 g) delivers approximately 1089 kcal, 39.6 g protein, 42.9 g fat, and 139.7 g carbohydrate. Clinically, the platter is moderate-GI (composite GI ~52) with a high glycemic load per full serving (~36), driven primarily by the Lebanese bread. Sodium content is elevated (~1705 mg per serving), making portion control important for individuals managing hypertension or cardiovascular disease. This dish is vegetarian and halal-suitable. It is commonly served as a restaurant combo meal in Indian vegetarian restaurants in Singapore that offer cross-cultural menus.

confidence_reason: No single approved source covers this exact three-component fusion platter. Nutrient values are weighted composites derived from ICMR-NIN IFCT 2017 (mutter paneer component) and USDA SR Legacy (hummus and Lebanese bread components), all approved primary sources. GI is an analogous estimate from University of Sydney GI Database and Atkinson et al. 2008. Confidence is medium rather than high because the composite estimation introduces uncertainty in exact proportions as served by the specific restaurant.
notes: 1. This record represents a restaurant combo platter (GD002064) sold at Jewel of India, Boat Quay, Singapore, combining one portion of mutter paneer, one portion of hummus, and two pieces of Lebanese bread. 2. Serving weight of 550 g is an estimate based on typical restaurant portions: mutter paneer ~200 g, hummus ~100 g, Lebanese bread 2 × ~125 g = 250 g. 3. Per-100g nutrient values are weighted composites: mutter paneer 36.4% weight share (ICMR-NIN IFCT 2017), hummus 18.2% weight share (USDA SR Legacy #16167), Lebanese bread 45.5% weight share (USDA SR Legacy #18033). 4. vitamin_d_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in ICMR-NIN IFCT 2017 for mutter paneer, and the USDA SR Legacy entries for hummus and pita bread do not provide a reliable composite value for this nutrient. 5. selenium_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in ICMR-NIN IFCT 2017 for this dish category, and reliable composite data are unavailable. 6. trans_fat_g is 0.0 as no trans fats are present in the natural ingredients (paneer, peas, chickpeas, tahini, olive oil, wheat flatbread) under standard preparation; confirmed negligible per USDA SR Legacy source data. 7. GI composite calculation: mutter paneer GI ~41 (analogous to vegetable curry with legumes, Atkinson et al. 2008), hummus GI ~6 (University of Sydney GI Database), pita/Lebanese bread GI ~57 (University of Sydney GI Database); weighted by carbohydrate contribution per component. 8. Multilingual names provided for Chinese (simplified and traditional), Malay, Tamil, and Hindi as required for Singapore-served dishes. 9. Food group retained as 'Meat and alternatives' per input, consistent with paneer as the primary protein source; hummus (chickpeas) also falls within this group. 10. Price data (SGD 22.89) preserved from GrabFood source for reference only; not used in nutritional calculations.

improvement_actions_taken: Cleaned input food_name from menu-style '1XMutter Paneer +1X Hummus with 2Pcs of Lebanese Bread' to canonical 'Mutter Paneer with Hummus and Lebanese Bread'.
Constructed composite nutrient profile from ICMR-NIN IFCT 2017 and USDA SR Legacy using documented weight proportions.
Added mandatory multilingual names for Chinese (simplified and traditional), Malay, Tamil, and Hindi.
Computed weighted composite GI from University of Sydney GI Database and Atkinson et al. 2008 peer-reviewed source.
Documented -1 sentinel values for vitamin_d_mcg and selenium_mcg with explicit source justification in notes.
Documented trans_fat_g 0.0 as confirmed negligible per source data.
Verified aliases_by_language and flat aliases consistency — all entries cross-checked.
Set ontology match_type values exclusively to permitted values: closest_parent and none.
Added specific clinical guidance for all three health conditions.
